concept: Find / Discover / Come Upon the Sought or Unsought
description: To find: the key that was lost, the path through the forest, the word that was wanted. Discovery, whether after long search or by grace of accident. Pairs with 'thilou' (inquire — seeking by asking) and 'sahu' (forget) as its shadow.
sound_symbolism: Three syllables trace the whole event: 'he', the searching breath; 'ka', the click of contact; 'wi', the brightness of the found thing in the hand. The word arrives the way finding does.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia kira to hekawi.' (I found the key.) Of discoveries: 'lo mia phelora ruela hekawi.' (We find a beautiful path.) The perfective marks the completed find: 'mia ki hekawi.' (I have found it.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: What is sought with grasping hides; what is sought with open attention is found. The word's own arc — breath before click — keeps the order honest.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The forager's whole craft: knowing where to look, and recognizing the found thing when it appears.
